headline: Hungary +9.4 — largest positive delta this rotation; first EU-cluster positive signal. Senegal band crossing: Functional → Developing on anti-LGBTQ+ law. Trilateral ceasefire day 1: methodology call distinguishes US-brokered format from prior unilateral collapses.
lead: Peter Magyar sworn in as Hungary's PM on May 9, ending Orbán's 16-year rule. First-day enacted actions on five of eight dimensions produce a +9.4 upgrade — the largest single-cycle positive movement in the countries index this rotation. Simultaneously, Senegal receives its first baseline assessment and crosses downward from Functional to Developing (-10.9) on a March 2026 anti-LGBTQ+ law that doubled prison sentences, criminalized civil society support, and is documented to be disrupting HIV services. The trilateral Russia-Ukraine ceasefire (day 1 of 3) introduces a structural format distinction from the May 5-7 unilateral collapses: US-brokered, dual presidential confirmation, partial compliance observed — but Russia's Kharkiv residential drone strike on ceasefire day 1 (5 injured including two 8-year-olds) registers as a documented breach. Ukraine earns conditional day-1 credit; Russia's breach record extends.

scoreChanges.bandChangeNote: Developing retained. 2.5 points below Functional floor (40.0). Five dimensions credited +0.5 each for enacted inaugural actions. Full band crossing deferred pending 30-day re-assessment June 9.
scoreChanges.headline: Hungary — HOLD RELEASED. Peter Magyar sworn in May 9. First-day enacted actions: EU flag flown at Parliament (SYS/BND); TV2 news director and pro-Orbán presenters dismissed (ACC/SYS); Sulyok dismissal demanded with May 31 hard deadline (ACC); €17B frozen EU funds named top priority with 27 EU rule-of-law supermilestones agreed pre-inauguration (ACC/BND/INT); inaugural address: 'system change / service not rule' framing. Five dimensions credited +0.5 each. Proposed 37.5 (Developing). First positive EU-cluster signal this rotation. Largest single-cycle positive delta in countries index this rotation.
keyEvidence: EU flag flown at Parliament on inauguration day — enacted symbolic institutional realignment (SYS/BND)
TV2 news director dismissed, pro-Orbán presenters removed within hours of swearing-in (ACC/SYS)
Sulyok dismissal demanded with hard May 31 deadline — enacted accountability posture (ACC)
€17B frozen EU funds named top legislative priority; 27 rule-of-law supermilestones agreed with EU Commission; cooperative framework confirmed (ACC/BND/INT)
Inaugural address framed as 'system change / service not rule' — SYS register-level commitment signal
scoreChanges.triggerContext: Hold released by swearing-in event. May 3 assessment (29.7) held INT/ACC/EQU pending enacted reform. Day-1 actions fired five carry-over triggers simultaneously. Hold protocol: released on mandatory trigger, assessed as first-queue item.
watchItems: Sulyok dismissal compliance by May 31 deadline
EU fund-release milestone progress (27 supermilestones, August 31 deadline)
Cabinet consolidation and first legislative actions
Media freedom reform implementation (80% of Hungarian media under prior Orbán control)
30-day re-assessment 2026-06-09 — Functional band crossing (40.0+) decision point

scoreChanges.bandChangeNote: BAND CROSSING: Functional → Developing. Proposed score 37.5 is 2.5 below Functional floor (40.0). Clean band crossing with margin.
scoreChanges.stagingCorrectionNote: Initially staged as 40.6 boundary case. Dimension-level math reconstruction yielded 37.5 (3.1-point correction). Converted from near-boundary sub-threshold result to clean band crossing. Assessor math-discipline catch documented — second consecutive cycle with staging discrepancy caught at reconstruction.
scoreChanges.headline: Senegal — FIRST BASELINE. BAND CROSSING: Functional → Developing. Published composite 48.4 set before structural EQU regression. March 11, 2026: anti-LGBTQ+ law signed — doubled prison sentences (5-10 years), criminalized civil society support for LGBTQ+ persons, criminalized HIV transmission. 70+ arrests Feb-March 2026. First conviction April 10 (6 years). UN High Commissioner Türk urged president not to sign — signed despite objection (ACC). New Humanitarian May 5, 2026 in-window: law disrupting HIV services (EMP). 959+ migrants dead at sea Jan-May 2026 (secondary BND signal). Proposed 37.5 / Developing.
keyEvidence: March 11, 2026: Anti-LGBTQ+ law signed — prison sentences doubled to 5-10 years, HIV transmission criminalized, civil society support criminalized (EQU)
70+ arrests February-March 2026; first conviction April 10, 2026 (6-year sentence) — enacted enforcement, not dormant statute (EQU/ACC)
UN High Commissioner Türk publicly urged president not to sign; law signed despite objection — documented refusal of multilateral human-rights guidance (ACC)
New Humanitarian May 5, 2026 in-window report: law actively disrupting HIV services — documented EMP harm pathway (EMP)
959+ migrants dead at sea from Senegalese coast January-May 2026 (BND secondary signal)
scoreChanges.triggerContext: First baseline. Published 48.4 set before March 2026 anti-LGBTQ+ law. New evidence: enacted criminalization structure, UN objection ignored, documented harm to HIV services.
watchItems: Government response to international condemnation following band-crossing publication

confirmations.headline: Ukraine — EMERGENCY RE-QUEUE. Trilateral ceasefire day-1 conduct. Zelenskyy acknowledged Russia 'reduced large-scale missile and aerial bombardments' — partial compliance observed, structurally distinct from May 5-7 total-collapse pattern. Ukraine conduct contains no attributable day-1 ceasefire breaches. Conditional credit granted. Score held at 50.0 / Functional. Full scoring outcome deferred to May 11 (day 3 + prisoner exchange). 1,000-prisoner swap preparation ongoing.
confirmations.triggerContext: Day 1 of US-brokered trilateral ceasefire (May 9-11). Dual presidential confirmation. Ukraine strike on Yaroslavl oil facility framed as retaliation for Russian city attacks — not attributed as ceasefire breach. Mandatory re-queue for May 10-11.
watchItems: May 10: ceasefire day 2 conduct — Russia compliance pattern
May 11: ceasefire day 3 + 1,000-prisoner exchange completion deadline
Full INT scoring outcome post-day-3 evidence
ACT signal from prisoner exchange completion or failure

floorEntities.headline: Russia — FLOOR CONDUCT DOCUMENTATION. No score change. Two new conduct categories documented: (1) North Korean troops publicly displayed at Victory Day parade for the first time — SYS normalization of DPRK military partnership; institutionalization moved from battlefield deployment to official state ceremony. (2) Kharkiv residential building drone strike on ceasefire day 1 (May 9 evening) — 5 injured including two 8-year-old boys; Russia's Defense Ministry simultaneously claimed 'strict observance' — direct bad-faith-INT conduct event. Putin held scaled-down parade (no heavy weapons — 'needed at the front'); declared war 'coming to an end'; framed conflict as fighting 'NATO-backed aggressive force.' DATA INTEGRITY: rotation-state.json shows 6.3 vs canonical 0.0 floor — sync action required.
conductCategories: North Korean troops publicly displayed at Victory Day parade — first official state normalization of DPRK military partnership (SYS)
Kharkiv residential building drone strike on trilateral ceasefire day 1 (May 9 evening) — 5 injured including two 8-year-olds; claimed 'strict observance' simultaneously (INT/EMP)
Victory Day speech: framed conflict as 'NATO-backed aggressive force' — continued external-escalation narrative (BND)
Continued front-line offensive operations on advancing sectors during ceasefire window (IHL/EMP)
floorEntities.dataIntegrityFlag: rotation-state.json composite 6.3 vs canonical 0.0 — sync action required. Same class as Pakistan flag May 8.

floorEntities.headline: Israel — FLOOR CONDUCT DOCUMENTATION. No score change. New conduct category: humanitarian interdiction in international waters with reported torture/inhumane treatment — distinct from Gaza military operations category. OHCHR May 8, 2026 press release (in-window): demands immediate release of two flotilla activists detained in Israel. April 29 maritime interception: 22 boats stopped in international waters (500 nautical miles from Israeli coast), 180 civilians detained. 35 individuals described severe mistreatment and sexual assault to UN experts — may amount to torture under UNCAT. Thiago Ávila (Brazilian) and Saif Abu Keshek (Spanish-Swedish) remain in Israeli custody in isolation. OHCHR cited UNCLOS violations, arbitrary detention, and possible torture/inhumane treatment. Appended to floor-exit-criteria record as new conduct category.
conductCategories: April 29, 2026 maritime interception in international waters (500 nm from Israeli coast) — 22 boats, 180 civilians detained (UNCLOS violation)
35 individuals reported severe mistreatment and sexual assault — may amount to torture or inhumane treatment (UNCAT)
Two activists (Brazilian and Spanish-Swedish nationals) transferred to Israel, held in isolation, arbitrary detention (arbitrary detention protocols)
OHCHR May 8 in-window press release: demands immediate release — institutional documentation event
floorEntities.conductCategoryDistinction: Humanitarian-interdiction with reported torture allegations in international waters is a qualitatively distinct conduct category from the Gaza military operations already in the floor record. Different jurisdiction (UNCLOS), different victim population (international humanitarian volunteers), different legal framework (arbitrary detention, UNCAT).

mathHygiene.note: Math-hygiene cluster unchanged at 13 entities. No new flags this cycle. All cycle counts incremented by 1. Cross-index formula audit (robotics-labs + ai-labs composite-derivation formulas) remains the highest-priority unresolved data-team action. No Established/Exemplary boundary composites in either index should be published or revised until audit is complete.

criticalFlag.severity: CRITICAL — 7 cycles carried; action critically overdue. Largest discrepancy in cluster. Published score at risk of external citation as valid benchmark data.

mathHygiene.auditAction: Data-team bulk audit of robotics-labs AND ai-labs composite-derivation formulas — CRITICAL. Must move from flagged to in-progress this cycle. Open Bionics 7-cycle carry is publication-integrity risk.
